commit.message:
fix: queue messages during session creation to prevent silent loss (#287)

## Bug
New sessions open empty with no dialog — user's first message is
silently lost.

## Root Cause
Race condition between optimistic session creation and user interaction:

1. `CreateSessionAsync` adds session to UI immediately with `IsCreating
= true`
2. SDK session creation is awaited (takes seconds)
3. During this window, user can type and send messages
4. `SendPromptAsync` throws `InvalidOperationException` ("Session is
still being created")
5. Error is silently swallowed in fire-and-forget `ContinueWith`
6. Input text is already cleared before send — message is lost
7. User sees empty session with no dialog

Secondary: `ChatDatabase.AddMessageAsync` crash in crash.log due to
missing fields in `ChatMessageEntity`.

## Fix
1. **Queue messages when `IsCreating` is true** (Dashboard.razor) — same
pattern as existing `IsProcessing` queue logic
2. **Drain queue after session creation completes** (CopilotService.cs)
— sends first queued message once SDK session is ready
3. **Show error feedback in chat** (Dashboard.razor) — display errors as
error messages in chat instead of only logging to console
4. **Add missing DB fields** (ChatDatabase.cs) — `ToolInput`,
`ImagePath`, `ImageDataUri`, `Caption` added to `ChatMessageEntity` with
round-trip support

## Tests
- 13 new tests in `NewSessionEmptyDialogTests.cs`: IsCreating state,
queue behavior, round-trip entity conversion, error messages
- 2 new integration tests in `ChatDatabaseResilienceTests.cs`: DB
round-trip for ToolInput and Image fields
- All 1980 existing tests continue to pass

Add one-click LLM efficiency analysis for sessions (#244)

## Summary

Add a "📊 Analyze Efficiency" button to the session context menu that
runs a one-click LLM efficiency analysis in a new temporary Copilot
session.

## How it works

1. **Click** the ⋯ menu on any session → "📊 Analyze Efficiency"
2. **C# extractor** (`SessionMetricsExtractor`) parses `events.jsonl` +
process logs in-process — no Python, no external dependencies
3. **Metrics JSON** is saved to
`{sessionDir}/files/efficiency-metrics.json` so the analysis session can
read/grep/process it with tools
4. **New Copilot session** is created with the full analysis skill
prompt (waste patterns, report template, cost methodology, baselines,
reduction strategies) and auto-sent
5. **Analysis session** opens automatically — Copilot reads the metrics
file and produces a comprehensive efficiency report

## What the report includes

- LLM usage summary (calls, tokens, cache rate, cost, duration)
- Dev loop summary (builds, tests, fix cycles, redundant builds)
- LLM call distribution by model
- Call breakdown and categorization by output size
- Efficiency verdict with emoji assessment
- Up to 5 quantified waste findings with reduction strategies
- Cost estimate with current vs optimized pricing
- Baseline comparison table

## Files

### New
- **`PolyPilot/Services/SessionMetricsExtractor.cs`** — Pure C# port of
the Python extraction script. Parses `events.jsonl` (session start, user
messages, turns, tool calls, subagents, compactions, errors, dev loop)
and process logs (LLM call telemetry). Includes model pricing table and
cost estimation.
- **`PolyPilot/Services/EfficiencyAnalysisService.cs`** — Orchestrates
analysis: extracts metrics, writes JSON to disk, creates session, sends
prompt with full skill instructions from
[llm-efficiency-skill](https://github.com/mattleibow/llm-efficiency-skill)
- **`PolyPilot.Tests/SessionMetricsExtractorTests.cs`** — 15 unit tests
covering all event types, dev loop detection, error handling, edge cases

### Modified
- **`SessionListItem.razor`** — "📊 Analyze Efficiency" menu item +
`OnAnalyze` EventCallback
- **`SessionSidebar.razor`** — Inject `EfficiencyAnalysisService`, wire
callback, switch to analysis session
- **`MauiProgram.cs`** — Register `EfficiencyAnalysisService` singleton
- **`PolyPilot.Tests.csproj`** — Compile include for
`SessionMetricsExtractor.cs`

## Testing

- 15 new unit tests all passing

fix: preserve DevTunnel error message when HostAsync fails (#283)

## Problem
When clicking "Start Dev Tunnels", the button briefly flashes a spinner
then returns to its initial state with **no error message** — no QR code
appears and no explanation is shown.

## Root Cause
In `DevTunnelService.HostAsync()`, when `TryHostTunnelAsync()` fails
(both initial and retry attempts), `Stop()` is called for cleanup at
line 237. `Stop()` transitions through `Stopping → NotStarted`, and
`SetState(NotStarted)` clears `_errorMessage` (since only the `Error`
state preserves it). The error that was set by `SetError()` during the
failed attempt is wiped before the UI can display it.

The UI then shows the start button again (state = `NotStarted`) instead
of the error panel (state = `Error`).

## Fix
1. **Preserve error across `Stop()`**: Save `_errorMessage` before
`Stop()`, then re-set it via `SetError()` after cleanup so the user sees
what went wrong.
2. **Catch block cleanup**: Call `Stop()` before `SetError()` in the
catch block so the WsBridge started earlier in the method is properly
cleaned up on unexpected exceptions.

## Tests Added
- `Stop_ClearsErrorMessage_ByDesign` — confirms Stop() clears error
(documents the root cause behavior)
- `HostAsync_WhenTunnelFails_PreservesErrorMessage` — end-to-end:
verifies failed HostAsync ends in Error state with a message
- `ErrorPreservation_SaveAndRestore_AcrossStop` — tests the save/restore
pattern used in the fix
- `SetError_SetsStateToError_AndPreservesMessage` — verifies SetError
behavior
- `SetError_FiresOnStateChanged` — verifies event fires

All 85 DevTunnel tests pass. All 1970 passing tests remain green (13
pre-existing PopupThemeTests failures unrelated).

feat: add appendix-a-math project for Mathematical Background (Appendix A)

Implements the interactive visualization project for Appendix A — Mathematical
Background from AIMA 4th Ed., following the same structure as existing chapters.

## What's included

### §A.1 Complexity Analysis
- compareComplexities: compare all 8 classes at a given n
- isBigOValid: verify f(n) ≤ c·g(n) for all n ≥ n0
- findBigOConstants: find minimal c and n0 for a Big-O relationship

### §A.2 Linear Algebra
- addVectors, scaleVector, dotProduct
- matMul: arbitrary m×k × k×n matrix multiplication
- transpose: m×n → n×m
- applyTransform: 2×2 matrix applied to 2D point
- eigenvalues2x2: real and complex eigenvalues via characteristic polynomial

### §A.3 Probability Distributions
- gaussianPDF / gaussianCDF (Abramowitz & Stegun erf approximation)
- uniformPDF / uniformCDF
- bernoulliPMF, binomialPMF (log-space), poissonPMF (log-space)
- exponentialPDF / exponentialCDF
- betaPDF (log-space, with boundary cases), logGamma (Lanczos approximation)
- sampleMean, sampleVariance (Bessel's correction)

## Testing
- 113 tests, 100% branch + line coverage (vitest + v8)
- All edge cases: n=0, empty arrays, boundary distributions, complex eigenvalues

Add GeoPackage (.gpkg) export support in download panel

Stream filtered spatial data from DuckDB (Arrow batches via conn.send)
into wa-sqlite running in a Web Worker with OPFSAdaptiveVFS. Writes a

- Required metadata tables (gpkg_spatial_ref_sys, gpkg_contents,
  gpkg_geometry_columns, gpkg_extensions)
- R-tree spatial index (rtree_features_geom)
- GeoPackageBinary geometry encoding (GP header + WKB)
- PRAGMA application_id/user_version per spec
- Smart geometry type promotion (LINESTRING+MULTILINESTRING -> MULTI)

Uses custom wa-sqlite build from ramseraph/sqwab with SQLITE_ENABLE_RTREE.

commit.message:
test(engine): add deep watchdog and safety interlock tests (#178)

Add 57 comprehensive tests for watchdog and safety interlock subsystems:

- Concurrent SharedWatchdog access (thread-safe feed, arm/disarm, timeout visibility)
- Watchdog behavior during recovery sequences (timeout during fault, reset/rearm)
- Rapid state oscillation (fault/clear cycles, challenge/cancel cycles, estop/reset)
- Simultaneous/cascading faults (multi-fault logging, fault during challenge/ack)
- Timing guarantees (fault-to-zero <10ms, timeout response <1ms, estop <1ms)
- Challenge-response edge cases (wrong tokens, missing combo start, short hold, expiry)
- Safety state machine invariants (torque limits per state, hands-off timeout)
- Communication loss edge cases (no initial report, refresh prevents loss)
- Torque limit edge cases (zero max, safe mode limit)
- WatchdogTimeoutHandler edge cases (double timeout, reset/reuse)
- Property-based tests with proptest (200 cases per property):
  - Any fault yields zero torque
  - Early clear_fault rejected
  - clear_fault restores SafeTorque
  - Faulted blocks high-torque request
  - Watchdog timeout produces zero torque and fault
  - Torque bounded in normal operation
  - Emergency stop always zero
  - Clamp idempotent
  - Cancel challenge from SafeTorque fails

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect per project policy).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove dying /bin/true procd instance that breaks UCI reload triggers (#30)

* fix: remove dummy /bin/true procd instance that causes service to die and breaks UCI triggers

The start_service() in /etc/init.d/tailscale-settings created a procd instance
running /bin/true with respawn 3600 1 3. Since /bin/true exits in milliseconds
(way below the 3600s threshold), every exit counted as a crash. After 3 crashes,
procd marks the service as dead and may stop processing its UCI config-change
triggers (registered via procd_add_reload_trigger), meaning LuCI Save & Apply
never called reload_service() → apply_settings() → tailscale set.

Fix: remove the procd instance. tailscale-settings is a config-only service.
The trigger registration happens in _procd_close_service() via service_triggers()
regardless of whether any procd instances exist.
